message:
feat(vendored/mux_client): tick 154 unix_stream_read_with_cx + read_next_pdu_with_cx routing (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Fifth post-storage tick. vendored/mux_client.rs had 31 `_with_cx`
methods but 4 naive "missing" — 2 were non-asupersync-only
(`join_subscription_task`, `run_subscription_loop`), 1 was
test-only (`pane_delta_send`), leaving 1 real target:
`unix_stream_read`.

New sibling:
- unix_stream_read_with_cx — pre-flight `cx.checkpoint()` folded
  into `io::ErrorKind::Interrupted` so a cancelled caller surfaces
  as an IO error rather than blocking on the underlying
  `poll_read`. This matches the existing convention in the mux
  client where cancellation propagates through `DirectMuxError::Io`.

Call-site migration in `read_next_pdu_with_cx` (L1705):
- Line 1725 (was `unix_stream_read`) →
  `unix_stream_read_with_cx(cx, &mut self.stream, &mut temp)`

This is the response-read phase of PDU decoding; the prior
`checkpoint_mux_cx` call two lines earlier already gates entry to
the loop iteration, but the new sibling adds a second gate
immediately before the kernel-level read so a concurrent cancel
between the loop-entry checkpoint and the actual read now surfaces
as ErrorKind::Interrupted rather than waiting for the read to
return data.

Legacy `read_next_pdu` (L1638) preserved; its call site at L1656
still routes through the ambient `unix_stream_read`.

Verified via fork bypass: 109/109 vendored::mux_client:: tests pass,
including `await_response_with_precancelled_cx_fails_before_reading_frame`
which transitively covers the response-read cancel path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
